description: Phase 0 vectors must not claim certified maturity. Structural, not just semantic: manifest.schema.json's document.maturity enum is [presence-only, hash-checked, realized-edge-observed] and has never included any certified-tier value, so this also rejects the two CS-1.4 tiers (parity-certified, authority-certified) the same way — a document declaration cannot claim any of them at Phase 0, by construction. This vector keeps exercising the bare 'certified' id as the representative case; the phase0_disallowed set in maturity-labels.json now lists all three ids for the semantic layer's benefit.

findings.message: Certified maturity is reserved for post-XG-1 evidence.
